book_claims.claim: Regular legume consumption (3+ servings/week) associated with longest lifespan among Loma Linda Adventists
book_claims.mechanism: High fiber, plant protein, and complex carbohydrates; low glycemic load; does not activate pro-aging pathways like animal protein

book_claims.claim: Legumes are a core food in all centenarian blue-zone diets (Okinawa, Sardinia, Calabria, Loma Linda, Costa Rica, Greece)
book_claims.mechanism: Plant protein without IGF-1-elevating effect of animal protein; high fiber for gut health

book_claims.claim: Beans eaten almost every day by Molochio centenarians, especially in traditional dishes with vegetables
book_claims.mechanism: High plant protein, fiber, and complex carbohydrates without animal-protein IGF-1 effects

book_claims.claim: Legumes are a core component of the Longevity Diet and associated with healthy aging, protection from cardiovascular disease, and cancer prevention
book_claims.mechanism: High in plant-based protein, complex carbohydrates, fiber, vitamins, and minerals; low in saturated fat; cause a lower insulin release than processed carbohydrates

book_claims.claim: Intake of beans reduces the risk of heart disease in women
book_claims.mechanism: Plant protein and soluble fiber lower LDL cholesterol and reduce cardiovascular risk

supplementary_research.finding: Fava bean consumption is associated with reduced all-cause mortality in cohort studies of Blue Zone populations, particularly in Sardinia and Calabria (PMID 24898241)
Fava beans are rich in L-DOPA, which supports dopaminergic function, and their fiber fraction significantly attenuates postprandial glucose response (PMID 31619630)

Fava beans provide 26 g protein per 100 g dry weight, with a unique profile including good amounts of L-DOPA, folate, and manganese; their high fiber content (25 g per 100 g dry) strongly supports gut microbiome diversity

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Plant protein with good digestibility; lower leucine content than animal protein reduces IGF-1 activation
Water-soluble; essential for DNA methylation and cell division; highly bioavailable from legumes
Cofactor for superoxide dismutase (MnSOD), the primary mitochondrial antioxidant enzyme
Combination of soluble and insoluble fiber; supports SCFA production and gut microbiome diversity

synergies.description: Olive oil's oleic acid promotes satiety and reduces the glycemic response; traditional Italian fave e cicoria (fava beans with chicory) exemplifies this pairing
Vitamin C from lemon enhances non-heme iron absorption from fava beans; also brightens flavor
Fava beans and farro together create a nutritionally complete protein profile, echoing the traditional Calabrian pasta e fagioli longevity diet
